title: Stenia falcata
taxonomy: Stenia falcata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the orchid family, specifically within the subtribe Spiranthinae. It belongs to the genus Stenia and the family Orchidaceae. The subtribe Spiranthinae is part of the tribe Cranichideae. This classification is based on the plant's physical characteristics and genetic makeup. The Orchidaceae family is the third-largest family of flowering plants, with over 30,000 known species.

intro: Stenia falcata, also known as Stinging Nettle, is a perennial plant that belongs to the nettle family (Urticaceae). The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, and its health benefits include reducing inflammation, relieving arthritis symptoms, and aiding in digestion. The bioactive constituents of Stenia falcata include flavonoids, saponins, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal preparations, Stenia falcata is commonly found in teas, infusions, capsules, and creams, where it is often used to alleviate joint pain, improve skin health, and support immune function.
